content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both touch quantize() so full file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal to minimize collision risk: (1) In the quantize() signature, I'll append my new kwarg as the very LAST parameter: ...,dither=Dither.FLOYDSTEINBERG, palette=Palette.WEB, colors=256, sort_palette=None). Please add your error_threshold param BEFORE mine, right after the existing params, so our additions land on adjacent-but-distinct tokens in the same line rather than both racing for 'last'. (2) I'll put my sorting logic in a small new block placed immediately before the final 'return im' statement, guarded by 'if sort_palette:' - I won't touch any other existing lines/logic in the method. Please put your error_threshold logic in a separate block elsewhere (e.g. right after quantization result is obtained, before mine) so blocks don't interleave. (3) In tests, I'll only ADD new test functions (test_sort_palette_*) without modifying existing tests - please do the same with distinct names for yours. Let me know if this works or propose adjustments, then we can both ACCEPT.
